sections.paragraph:
διερμηνευτής, οῦ, ὁ (s. prec. entry; does not occur again until the Byz. gramm. Eustath., Ad Il. p. 106, 14; Nägeli 50) interpreter, translator of ecstatic speech 1 Cor 14:28 (s. ἑρμηνευτής).—M-M. TW. Spicq.

prec. prec. = preceding

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

gramm. gramm. (after a personal name)=grammaticus, grammarian

Eustath Eustath , XII a.d.—List 5

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 244
